title: berlandiera monocephala
intro: Berlandiera monocephala, also known as Mexican gold poppy, is a perennial flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues, including stomach ulcers and diarrhea. Berlandiera monocephala has been traditionally used to treat digestive problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which also provide health benefits such as soothing digestive discomfort and reducing inflammation. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although proper dosing and preparation should be consulted with a medical professional.
